entries.problem: Create array from list / pre-allocate buffer
entries.trigger: Converting Python data to NumPy or initializing a fixed-shape output
entries.snippet:
a = np.array([1, 2, 3], dtype=np.float32)
z = np.zeros((batch, features))
r = np.arange(0, 10, 2)
entries.minimal_notes: Use zeros not empty for pre-allocation — empty contains garbage values.
entries.common_bug: Mixed int/float list silently upcasts to float64, doubling memory for large arrays.

entries.problem: Reshape array for model input (batch, channel, height, width)
entries.trigger: Preparing a tensor shape mismatch before a layer forward pass
entries.snippet:
x = arr.reshape(batch, -1)           # flatten spatial dims
x = np.expand_dims(arr, axis=1)      # add channel dim: (N,H,W) -> (N,1,H,W)
x = np.transpose(arr, (0, 2, 1))     # swap last two dims
entries.minimal_notes: reshape returns a view when possible — call .copy() if you need an independent array.
entries.common_bug: Broadcasting (3,) + (3,1) silently produces (3,3) instead of element-wise — always verify .shape after operations.

entries.trigger: Masking padding tokens, selecting positive predictions, filtering by label
entries.snippet:
positive = arr[arr > 0]                        # boolean mask — returns 1D
replaced = np.where(arr > 0, arr, 0.0)         # conditional replace — preserves shape
selected = arr[[0, 3, 5]]                      # fancy index — returns copy
entries.minimal_notes: Boolean mask on 2D array always returns 1D flat result. Use np.where to preserve shape.
entries.common_bug: Fancy index assignment arr[[0,1]] = 5 may silently not apply — use direct slice assignment for in-place modification.

entries.problem: Compute per-axis statistics (sum, mean, std, argmax)
entries.trigger: Computing per-batch loss stats, class prediction indices, L2 norms for normalization
entries.snippet:
col_means = np.mean(arr, axis=0, keepdims=True)   # per-column, shape preserved
preds     = np.argmax(logits, axis=1)              # predicted class per row
norms     = np.linalg.norm(X, ord=2, axis=1, keepdims=True)
entries.minimal_notes: keepdims=True is essential to broadcast the result back against the original array.
entries.common_bug: np.mean on int dtype silently truncates in NumPy < 2.0 — always cast to float first.

entries.problem: Solve linear system or decompose matrix (SVD, eig)
entries.trigger: PCA, least-squares regression, normalizing embeddings, computing cosine similarity
entries.snippet:
x = np.linalg.solve(A, b)                       # Ax = b, stable
U, s, Vh = np.linalg.svd(X, full_matrices=False) # economy SVD
w, v = np.linalg.eigh(M)                         # symmetric matrix: real output
entries.minimal_notes: Never use linalg.inv to solve Ax=b — use linalg.solve. Use eigh not eig for symmetric matrices.
entries.common_bug: linalg.inv on near-singular matrix (cond > 1e12) returns garbage silently — check np.linalg.cond first.
